description_long: This object consists of two large, cylindrical blue barrels with black bases and rims, appearing to be made of plastic. The barrels have a weathered, stained surface with visible dark patches and marks, suggesting prolonged outdoor use. One of the barrels has a partially open lid with two fish tails protruding, indicating it may be used for storage or waste, possibly in a fishing or industrial context. The lids are flat and dark, with a slightly raised rim, and the barrels lack any visible handles or spouts. The overall construction appears robust, with reinforced bands at the top and bottom for structural integrity. The barrels are utilitarian in design, suitable for holding liquids, waste, or materials in urban or industrial environments.
description: Two large, blue, weathered plastic barrels with black bases and rims, one with a partially open lid and fish tails sticking out.
description_view_0: Front view showing both barrels, with the left barrel closed and the right barrel's lid partially open, revealing fish tails.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stained surface of the left barrel and the open lid with fish tails on the right barrel.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the open lid and protruding fish tails, with both barrels' tops clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the closed barrel's stained surface and the open barrel with fish tails from a different angle.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: plastic reinforcement rings at top and bottom
